stories.title: Cubs 9, Padres 7 — Game 2 Recap (June 30)
stories.angle: Dansby Swanson hit TWO home runs, Alex Bregman hit a three-run blast, Michael Busch and Pete Crow-Armstrong added solo HRs; five Cubs home runs total; Boyd (3-1) got the win; Cubs' fourth straight victory; 48-38; Padres on four-game losing streak

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Cubs 9, Padres 7.

Swanson went deep TWICE, Bregman hit a three-run blast, and Busch and Crow-Armstrong added solo HRs. Five home runs, four straight wins.

Boyd gets the W (3-1). This offense is dangerous.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
x_posts.tweet_type: Informative — Overnight game recap (7:00 AM) + Bold — Fan energy (8:15 AM)

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Dansby Swanson homered TWICE last night. The Cubs are 48-38, riding four straight wins, and hitting the ball like a team that means business.

July doesn't scare them.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball
x_posts.tweet_type: Informative — Overnight game recap (7:00 AM) + Bold — Fan energy (8:15 AM)

stories.title: Game 3 Preview — Rea vs. Buehler, Sweep in Sight
stories.angle: Colin Rea (4.80 ERA, better in last 2 starts) vs. Walker Buehler (1.71 ERA over last 5 starts, 1 run in EACH start); Cubs lead series 2-0 going for sweep; 1:20 PM CT day game at Wrigley; must preview before 10 AM per playbook

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Colin Rea takes the mound at 1:20 PM CT. Cubs go for the series sweep.

The challenge: Walker Buehler has allowed one run in EACH of his last five starts. A 1.71 ERA over that stretch. Rea needs to match zeros.

2-0 series lead. Let's close it.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Wild Card Watch — Cubs 48-38
stories.angle: Cubs firmly in NL Wild Card race at 48-38; four straight wins entering July; offensive firepower (five HRs last night) makes this team dangerous; rotation health remains the question

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
48-38. Firmly in the Wild Card race and riding four straight wins to open July.

The Cubs are built for the long game. Depth, rotation (when healthy), and a lineup that just hung five home runs on San Diego.

This race is on.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: All-Star — PCA Shut Out of Phase 2, Reserve Path Remains
stories.angle: Phase 2 All-Star voting closes July 2 at 12 PM ET; PCA finished 10th in Phase 1 and did not advance to final starter ballot; leads all MLB position players in WAR (4.6) and NL outfielders in OAA (15); reserve selection by players/commissioner is the only path now

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
All-Star Phase 2 voting closes tomorrow. PCA didn't make the final ballot.

He leads all MLB position players in WAR (4.6), leads NL outfielders in Outs Above Average (15), and still finished 10th in Phase 1 fan voting.

The reserves fix this.

#Cubs #GoCubs #ChicagoCubs

research.facts:
All-Star — PCA Phase 2
**Sources**:
- Bleacher Report Phase 1 results
- Sports Keeda Phase 2 finalists list
- SI.com (PCA WAR/OAA data)
- CBS Philadelphia (Phase 2 voting announcement)
- Bleacher Nation multiple articles
**Verified facts**:
- PCA finished 10th among NL outfielders in Phase 1 — HIGH (Bleacher Report article)
- PCA Phase 1 vote total: 1,061,310 — MEDIUM (single source)
- Phase 2 voting closes July 2 at 12 PM ET — HIGH (multiple sources)
- Votes did not carry over from Phase 1 to Phase 2 — HIGH (CBS Philadelphia)
- PCA did NOT advance to Phase 2 starter ballot (only top 6 per position advance) — HIGH (consistent across multiple sources)
- PCA WAR 4.6 leads all MLB position players (both bWAR and fWAR) — MEDIUM (described in multiple search summaries; compound superlative claim requiring cross-reference; directional confidence is high)
- PCA leads NL outfielders in Outs Above Average (15) — MEDIUM (SI.com article summary; directional)
- PCA leads NL outfielders in Fielding Run Value (16) — MEDIUM (same source; not used in tweet)
- PCA's path is as a reserve (commissioner + player votes) — HIGH (standard MLB All-Star process)
COMPOUND CLAIM FLAG: "Leads all MLB position players in WAR (4.6)" — this is a superlative claim. Multiple sources say it but I cannot verify against Baseball Reference same-day. Flagging as MEDIUM. Using it in tweet but including confidence note in fact-check log. The sources cite both bWAR and fWAR at 4.6 — the consistency across two metrics and multiple articles increases confidence.

stories.title: Roster Moves — Maton 15-Day IL, Milner Out 4-6 Weeks
stories.angle: Phil Maton placed on 15-day IL with right knee injury; Hobie Milner out 4-to-6 weeks following emergency appendectomy; two more bullpen arms down after Shaw + Roberts IL on June 29; further urgency for August 3 deadline

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
The Cubs' bullpen took two more hits.

Phil Maton lands on the 15-day IL with a right knee injury. Hobie Milner is out 4-to-6 weeks following an emergency appendectomy.

The rotation needs help. So does the pen. August 3 can't come soon enough.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

stories.title: Trade Deadline + Pipeline — 33 Days, Alcántara Odds Rise
stories.angle: 33 days to August 3; Cubs rotation ranks 27th in ERA (4.71); Sandy Alcántara trade odds hit 40% (Cubs co-frontrunner with Padres); Jaxon Wiggins threw 3.2 scoreless at High-A South Bend; Iowa next in rehab

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
33 days to the August 3 deadline.

Cubs rotation ranks 27th in ERA (4.71). Sandy Alcántara's trade odds hit 40%. Jaxon Wiggins just went 3.2 scoreless in High-A rehab — Iowa is next.

One deadline acquisition changes this race.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball
